title: Validation in Client/Server Applications
text: Validation in Client/Server Applications In client/server applications, most of the application processing occurs in the client machines, with a central database located on a server. The key features of validation in a client/server application are: Validation usually occurs locally on the client. Validation can involve both Uniface’s external data structure and the storage medium. For example, when validating a primary key, Uniface checks that the key is unique relative to database occurrences and also the non-database occurrences within the component’s data structure. When validation occurs locally, only the local external data structure is available for this check. Performance can benefit from conducting validation on the clients, if the validation process does not involve database I/O. If validation requires cross-network database I/O, validation results in a substantial network overhead. Key validation, for example, always requires database I/O. Was this topic helpful?
